clause_text: The Recipient shall (a) use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; (b) protect the Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care it uses for its own confidential information of similar sensitivity, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care; and (c) limit access to the Confidential Information to employees, contractors, and advisors who have a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those in this Agreement.

obligations: protect with at least the same care as own confidential information of similar sensitivity (minimum reasonable care)
limit access to need-to-know personnel under equivalent confidentiality

clause_text: This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for a period of three (3) years thereafter, unless earlier terminated by either Party upon thirty (30) days' written notice. The obligations in Section 3 shall survive termination of this Agreement for an additional period of five (5) years with respect to trade secrets, which shall remain protected for so long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.

obligations: termination on 30 days' written notice by either Party
trade secrets protected for so long as they qualify

clause_text: Upon written request of the Discloser or upon termination of this Agreement, the Recipient shall promptly return or, at the Discloser's option, destroy all Confidential Information in its possession and certify such destruction in writing within ten (10) business days.

obligations: return or destroy CI at Discloser's option upon request or termination
written certification of destruction within 10 business days

clause_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflicts-of-laws principles. The Parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the state and federal courts located in Wilmington, Delaware.

obligations: exclusive jurisdiction in Wilmington, DE state/federal courts

clause_text: Orbit will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain a monthly uptime of 99.9% (the "Uptime SLA"), measured per calendar month and excluding scheduled maintenance announced at least 48 hours in advance. If actual uptime falls below the Uptime SLA, Customer shall be entitled, as its sole and exclusive remedy, to service credits as follows: (a) 99.0% to 99.89%: 5% credit of the monthly fee; (b) 95.0% to 98.99%: 10% credit of the monthly fee; (c) below 95.0%: 25% credit of the monthly fee.

obligations: tiered service credits as sole and exclusive remedy

clause_text: Customer shall pay the fees set forth in the Order Form. Unless otherwise specified, fees are due net thirty (30) days from invoice date. Late payments shall accrue interest at the lesser of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law. All fees are exclusive of taxes, and Customer is responsible for all applicable taxes other than taxes based on Orbit's net income.

obligations: Customer pays taxes other than Orbit's net-income tax

clause_text: As between the Parties, Customer retains all rights, title, and interest in and to Customer Data. Customer grants Orbit a limited, non-exclusive license to host, process, and display Customer Data solely as necessary to provide the Service. Orbit shall not use Customer Data to train or improve any model that is offered to third parties without Customer's prior written consent.

obligations: limited non-exclusive license to Orbit to host/process/display for Service
no third-party-model training without prior written consent

clause_text: EXCEPT FOR (i) BREACHES OF CONFIDENTIALITY, (ii) INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, AND (iii) WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, EACH PARTY'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CUSTOMER TO ORBIT IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE LIABILITY. NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES.

obligations: aggregate liability capped at trailing-12-month fees

clause_text: Orbit will defend Customer against any third-party claim alleging that Customer's authorized use of the Service infringes such third party's intellectual-property rights, and will indemnify Customer against damages and reasonable attorneys' fees finally awarded against Customer in such claim or paid in settlement approved in writing by Orbit. Orbit's obligations are conditioned on Customer providing prompt notice of the claim, granting Orbit sole control of the defense and settlement, and reasonably cooperating in the defense.

obligations: defense against third-party IP claims arising from authorized Service use
indemnification for awarded damages and approved settlements
Customer must provide prompt notice + sole control to Orbit + reasonable cooperation

clause_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement for cause if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after receiving written notice of the breach. Either Party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other Party becomes the subject of a bankruptcy petition that is not dismissed within sixty (60) days.

obligations: termination for material uncured breach after 30-day notice/cure
immediate termination on non-dismissed bankruptcy within 60 days

clause_text: Contractor shall provide consulting services to Company in the area of clinical-data integration as described in Exhibit A (the "Services"). Contractor will determine the manner and means by which the Services are performed, subject only to the reasonable directions of Company as to the results to be achieved.

obligations: Contractor controls manner/means; Company directs results

clause_text: The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for six (6) months unless earlier terminated as provided herein. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, upon fifteen (15) days' written notice to the other Party.

obligations: termination by either Party at any time on 15 days' written notice

clause_text: Company will pay Contractor an hourly rate of one hundred eighty U.S. dollars ($180.00 USD) for time actually spent performing the Services, capped at one hundred sixty (160) hours per month. Contractor shall submit monthly invoices itemizing the Services performed; Company shall pay each invoice within thirty (30) days of receipt. Contractor is responsible for all expenses incurred in the performance of the Services, except expenses pre-approved in writing by Company.

obligations: Contractor pays own expenses absent prior written approval

clause_text: Contractor is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, partner, or joint venturer of Company. Contractor is solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, and other statutory or contractual obligations of any sort. Contractor shall not be entitled to participate in any of Company's employee benefit plans.

obligations: no employment / agency / partnership / joint venture
Contractor pays own taxes and statutory obligations
no participation in Company benefit plans

clause_text: All deliverables, work product, inventions, discoveries, improvements, and other materials created by Contractor in the course of performing the Services (collectively, "Work Product") shall be deemed "works made for hire" to the extent permitted by law and shall be the sole and exclusive property of Company. To the extent any Work Product does not qualify as a work made for hire, Contractor hereby irrevocably assigns to Company all right, title, and interest in and to such Work Product, including all intellectual-property rights. Contractor shall execute all documents reasonably necessary to perfect Company's ownership.

obligations: Work Product = work-for-hire to extent permitted

clause_text: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months thereafter, Contractor shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or engagement any employee or contractor of Company with whom Contractor had material contact during the term.

obligations: no solicitation of Company employees or contractors
scope: those with material contact during term

clause_text: Contractor shall indemnify, defend, and hold Company harmless from and against any and all third-party claims, losses, damages, and reasonable attorneys' fees arising out of or relating to Contractor's breach of any representation, warranty, or covenant in this Agreement, including any claim that the Work Product infringes the intellectual-property rights of a third party.

obligations: scope: breaches of reps/warranties/covenants + IP infringement by Work Product

clause_text: You will be eligible to participate in the Company's annual performance bonus program with a target bonus of twenty percent (20%) of your base salary. The actual amount paid each year will be determined by the Company in its sole discretion based on individual and Company performance. To receive any annual bonus, you must be employed by the Company on the date the bonus is paid.

obligations: must be employed on payment date

clause_text: Subject to approval by the Board of Directors, you will be granted an option to purchase 35,000 shares of Lumen common stock at the fair market value on the date of grant. The option will vest over four (4) years, with twenty-five percent (25%) vesting on the first anniversary of your start date and the remaining seventy-five percent (75%) vesting monthly thereafter, subject to your continued service.

Your employment with the Company is "at-will," meaning that either you or the Company may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without cause and with or without notice. Nothing in this letter alters the at-will nature of your employment.

obligations: either party may terminate at any time, for any reason, with no notice

clause_text: If the Company terminates your employment without "Cause" (as defined in the Company's standard severance policy) outside of a change-of-control window, you will be eligible to receive six (6) weeks of base salary, paid in accordance with the Company's normal payroll practices, subject to your execution of a release of claims in a form provided by the Company.

obligations: no enhanced severance during CoC window per this clause

clause_text: During your employment and for a period of twelve (12) months thereafter, you shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit any employee of the Company to terminate his or her employment with the Company. You acknowledge that this restriction is reasonable and necessary to protect the Company's legitimate business interests.

obligations: no solicitation of Company employees to leave

clause_text: This offer is contingent on (a) satisfactory completion of standard background checks; (b) verification of your right to work in the United States in accordance with the Immigration Reform and Control Act of 1986; and (c) execution of all required new-hire documents.
As a condition of employment, you must sign the Company's standard Confidential Information and Invention Assignment Agreement on or before your first day of employment.

obligations: sign CIIAA on or before day 1 as condition of employment

clause_text: Out of scope: This contract does not contain any clause addressing this question.
